items.title: België wil met opmerkelijke pilot fans gecontroleerd vuurwerk af laten steken
items.summary: De Belgische overheid gaat de wetgeving omtrent illegaal vuurwerk in voetbalstadions strenger maken, maar start tegelijkertijd met een pilot om te onderzoeken of vuurwerk gecontroleerd afgestoken zou kunnen worden. Dit alleen door fans, die daarvoor speciaal zijn opgeleid. Het Belgische nazorg- en onderzoekscentrum voor mensen met brandwonden kan daar met de pet niet bij.

items.title: Nijhuis wil geen VAR-rol tijdens blessure: 'Kom het kantoor wel schoonmaken'
items.summary: Bas Nijhuis heeft geen behoefte aan een rol als VAR. De scheidsrechter heeft onlangs zijn achillespees gescheurd en is de komende maanden uit de roulatie. Bij Vandaag Inside vertelde Nijhuis dat de KNVB hem vervangend werk heeft voorgesteld, maar hij ziet een VAR-functie niet zitten.

items.title: Ex-England striker Carroll faces trial over alleged breach of non-molestation order
The goalscoring Spurs defender making headlines in Germany
items.summary: Luka Vuskovic has earned a reputation for scoring goals on loan at Hamburg, but just how good is the teenage Tottenham defender?

items.title: Scot Prem latest: Spurs move not right for Wilson, says Hearts boss
Cissè over 'geklapte' PSV-deal: 'Toen Milan kwam, twijfelde ik geen moment'
items.summary: Alphadjo Cissè heeft in gesprek met de Italiaanse krant La Repubblica kort teruggeblikt op zijn besluit om niet naar PSV te gaan, maar bij AC Milan te tekenen. De jonge Italiaan leek lange tijd op weg naar een contact bij PSV.

items.title: Nieuwe tactiek geeft Heracles hoop: 'Misschien is dit de oplossing'
items.summary: Heracles Almelo tankte afgelopen weekend vertrouwen met de eerste overwinning onder Ernest Faber. Eén zwaluw maakt nog geen zomer, maar de Almeloërs kwamen goed voor de dag tegen Fortuna Sittard. Met dank aan de rust van Remko Pasveer en de nieuwe tactiek van Faber. We zoomen in op de formatiewijziging.

items.title: 'He's better than that' - Wilson joining Spurs a bad decision, says McInnes
items.summary: Hearts manager Derek McInnes believes teenage striker James Wilson has made a mistake by joining Tottenham on loan.

items.title: Is Guardiola right about Man City's lower net spend?
items.summary: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola said his team only sit seventh on net spend over the last five years. Is he right?

items.title: The F1, football and NFL figures who kept Epstein links after conviction
items.summary: Senior figures in sport maintained personal contact with Jeffrey Epstein, while others were discussed regarding potential deals.

items.title: 'Al Hilal verlengt met Portugees international, nieuwe trainer bij Werder'
items.summary: In de buitenlandse topcompetities is Deadline Day voorbij, maar in enkele landen kunnen nog deals gesloten worden. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Transfer window verdict: how every Women’s Super League club fared
items.summary: After impressive work by Manchester United and Liverpool and disappointment for Chelsea, we assess every team’s business With so many senior players’ contracts expiring in June, Arsenal’s focus was on preparing for the summer, when they are expected to go through a major rebuild. Therefore their quiet window was no surprise, but they will be relatively pleased to have brought in a star of the future, Smilla Holmberg, at right-back and to have fulfilled their need for a backup goalkeeper, with Barbora Votíkova’s deadline-day loan. Much more significant, though, is the positive progress they are understood to have made in their attempt to sign Georgia Stanway on a free at the end of the season, and big decisions such as not seeking to extend Katie McCabe’s stay, as they prepare to refresh the team. Continue reading...

items.title: Arteta: 'Magical' final is 'best vitamins' for Arsenal
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has described Arsenal 's "magical" achievement in reaching the Carabao Cup Final as "the best vitamins we can put in our bodies."

items.title: Araujo hugs Flick after goal in 'step by step' return
items.summary: Hansi Flick said Barcelona will continue to support Ronald Araújo after the defender netted on his first start for the club since returning from a mental health break against Albacete in the Copa del Rey on Tuesday.

items.title: Fener ink Kanté deal after late Al Ittihad drama
items.summary: Fenerbahce have completed the signing of former Leicester and Chelsea midfielder N'Golo Kanté.

items.title: Benzema on new club Al Hilal: 'Madrid in Asia'
items.summary: Karim Benzema said he wants to bring trophies to his new club Al Hilal, which he called the "Real Madrid in Asia."

items.title: What wobble? Arsenal break hoodoo to reach first final in six years
items.summary: Arsenal will play in their first cup final in six years, aiming to put their recent lack of silverware behind them by lifting the Carabao Cup at Wembley.

items.title: Dubois vs Harper headlines all-female April show live on Sky Sports
items.summary: Caroline Dubois versus Terri Harper and Ellie Scotney’s undisputed title fight top an epic all-women’s TV bill on Sunday April 5 live on Sky Sports.

items.title: Everton sack Brian Sørensen with club languishing near bottom of WSL table
items.summary: Dane leaves after almost four years as manager Form at Goodison Park has been poor this season Everton have sacked women’s team manager Brian Sørensen with the club ninth in the Women’s Super League. The club claimed their first home victory of the season on Sunday to move four points clear of relegation danger but it has not been enough to keep the 45-year-old Dane, who has been in charge since 2022, in his role. The club’s chief executive, Angus Kinnear, said: “Sunday’s first win of the season at Goodison Park was important, but it has become evident that a change of direction is necessary to ensure the progression on the pitch we are all striving for.” Continue reading...

items.title: How did transfer window impact Championship promotion race?
items.summary: The January window is shut and the race for the Premier League is well and truly on.
